subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Fraction Fair team share snacks, label number lines, and show whole amounts as fractions.
lesson.mini_lesson: Fractions name equal parts of a whole or equal intervals on a number line.
lesson.objective: Understand fractions as equal parts and as numbers on a number line from 0 to 1.
lesson.concept_explanation: The denominator tells how many equal parts make the whole. The numerator tells how many of those parts are named. Fractions can also mark points on a number line.
lesson.common_misconception: Learners may count unequal parts, swap numerator and denominator, compare only denominator size, or miss that a whole can be written as n/n.
lesson.real_world_connection: Fractions help share food, measure lengths, read recipes, and describe parts of a whole.
lesson.lesson_summary: Check equal parts, name one unit fraction, count intervals on the number line, and write wholes as fractions.

worked_examples.prompt: The shape is split into equal halves. What fraction is shaded?
hints: Count equal parts first.
The denominator names the total equal parts.
The numerator tells how many parts are named.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model, then try again.

worked_examples.prompt: Write the unit fraction for 1 equal part out of 3.
hints: Count equal parts first.
The denominator names the total equal parts.
The numerator tells how many parts are named.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model, then try again.

worked_examples.prompt: On a number line from 0 to 1 split into 2 equal parts, what fraction is at tick 1?
hints: Count equal parts first.
The denominator names the total equal parts.
The numerator tells how many parts are named.

feedback.incorrect: Use the model, then try again.

game_theme.title: Fraction Fair
game_theme.name: Fraction Fair
game_theme.mission_intro: Open the Fraction Fair by partitioning wholes and placing fractions on number lines.
